purpose: Track software tasks that can be completed before physical hardware is ready.

hardware_status.note: Physical arm hardware is not yet ready for live testing. Prioritize mock-mode, fake-serial, config, calibration, preview, diagnostics, and test coverage work.

tasks.title: Add servo bus bring-up CLI scaffold
tasks.summary: Create a mock-safe CLI entrypoint for future STS3215 bring-up workflows.
acceptance_criteria: Adds a script entrypoint such as painterbot-bringup.
Supports mock/fake-safe commands for listing configured joints and intended servo IDs.
Does not require real serial hardware to run basic commands.
Has tests for argument parsing and mock-safe behavior.
evidence: Added painterbot-bringup entrypoint with mock-safe list-joints and protocols commands.
Added tests/test_bringup_cli.py covering argument parsing and no-serial behavior.

tasks.title: Design fake STS3215 serial harness
tasks.summary: Create a reusable fake serial object that can simulate STS3215 replies, corrupt packets, missing replies, stale acknowledgements, and multiple servo IDs.
acceptance_criteria: Reusable in unit tests outside tests/test_control.py.
Can serve position replies for multiple IDs.
Can simulate short reads, checksum errors, wrong IDs, and stale packets.
Documents which hardware behaviors are simulated versus still unknown.
evidence: Supports multi-ID position replies plus short read, checksum, wrong-ID, no-reply, and stale-packet simulations.
Documented simulated versus unknown hardware behavior in the fake harness module docstring.

tasks.title: Add servo ping/read preflight API
tasks.summary: Add a small service or function layer for reading configured servos and reporting actionable diagnostics.
acceptance_criteria: Can run against the fake STS3215 harness.
Reports missing feedback capability clearly.
Reports no reply, wrong ID, bad checksum, and success separately.
Does not move servos.
evidence: Classifies missing feedback, no reply, wrong ID, bad checksum, success, and unknown errors separately.
Uses only backend.read_servo, with tests confirming no goal-position writes occur.
Verified with .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_servo_preflight.py tests/test_fake_sts3215.py tests/test_control.py.

tasks.title: Create calibration session domain object
tasks.summary: Move required-pose workflow rules out of CLI conditionals into a testable calibration session abstraction.
acceptance_criteria: Knows required poses: home, pen_up, pen_down, corner_bl, corner_br, corner_tl, corner_tr.
Can capture a current arm pose under a required or custom name.
Reports missing poses and completion state.
Validates pose length and safe range consistency.
Covered by unit tests with mock arm/config objects.
evidence: Supports capture_current and capture for required or custom pose names.
Validates six numeric finite angles against configured joint safe ranges.

tasks.title: Add guided calibration dry-run command
tasks.summary: Add a non-hardware command path that shows the calibration steps, current completion state, and save target.
acceptance_criteria: Runs without hardware.
Prints required poses and whether each is present.
Identifies the workspace config file that would be used.
Reuses calibration session logic rather than duplicating rules.

tasks.title: Introduce DrawingPlan preflight object
tasks.summary: Unify dry-run, preview, and execution inputs around a single planned drawing representation.
acceptance_criteria: Stores fitted and resampled strokes.
Reports stroke count, point count, bounds, and estimated servo command count.
Can be generated without connecting to an arm.
Existing draw_shape and draw_svg dry-run outputs remain equivalent or improve.
Covered by tests for built-in shapes and SVG fixtures.

tasks.title: Add drawing preflight safety checks
tasks.summary: Fail before arm connection or movement when drawing, workspace, or calibration state is invalid.
acceptance_criteria: Detects missing calibration poses with exact missing names.
Detects paper margin leaving no usable area.
Detects empty drawings and degenerate path cases.
Confirms no serial writes occur on preflight failure.
Covered by tests.

tasks.title: Expand SVG fixture coverage
tasks.summary: Add representative SVG fixtures that stress lines, curves, multiple strokes, transforms, empty paths, and bounds.
acceptance_criteria: Adds fixtures under tests or examples.
Covers curves, multiple paths, transforms, and invalid/empty SVG behavior.
Documents any unsupported SVG features.
Keeps tests deterministic.

tasks.title: Add preview regression tests
tasks.summary: Strengthen PNG preview tests around paper bounds, drawing bounds, empty drawings, and output file creation.
acceptance_criteria: Tests preview output for representative shapes.
Checks generated image is non-empty.
Checks paper and drawing bounds are visible or encoded in predictable ways.
Avoids brittle pixel-perfect assertions unless needed.

tasks.title: Headless calibration UI testability pass
tasks.summary: Separate OpenCV click/UI concerns from homography and persistence logic so calibration can be tested without a display.
acceptance_criteria: Pure functions cover corner ordering, homography generation, and config update behavior.
OpenCV window code remains thin.
Tests run headlessly in CI/local terminal.
Failure messages explain bad corner counts or invalid image paths.

tasks.title: Add config validation for workspace pose shape
tasks.summary: Prevent malformed calibrated workspace files from reaching drawing execution.
acceptance_criteria: Required poses, when present, must have six numeric angles.
Errors identify the bad pose name and reason.
Existing unset default poses remain allowed.
Tests cover missing, null, too-short, too-long, and nonnumeric poses.

tasks.title: Add mock hardware session transcript command
tasks.summary: Create a command or documented script that demonstrates the planned no-hardware workflow from dry-run to mock execution.
acceptance_criteria: Shows setup, dry-run, preview, mock draw, and expected output.
Can be run repeatedly without modifying real configs unexpectedly.
Useful as a future agent handoff verification path.

tasks.title: Document hardware-arrival checklist as machine-trackable data
tasks.summary: Convert the hardware bring-up checklist into JSON or YAML so each physical step can be checked off later.
acceptance_criteria: Represents power, serial port, baud, servo IDs, safe ranges, and calibration poses.
Clearly marks which items require physical hardware.
Links back to docs/hardware_identification.md and docs/calibration.md.
Does not duplicate large prose blocks.

tasks.title: Add CI-friendly verification command docs
tasks.summary: Create a concise local verification checklist for future sessions that avoids the pyenv/python alias pitfall.
acceptance_criteria: Documents the exact pytest command using .venv/bin/python.
Includes dry-run and preview commands that do not require hardware.
Mentions optional scan extras separately.
Can be referenced from AGENT_HANDOFF.md.

tasks.title: Prototype scan import summary without execution
tasks.summary: Improve iPhone LiDAR import stubs so scan files can be summarized safely without any robot motion.
acceptance_criteria: Lazy-loads optional scan dependencies.
Reports mesh/file bounds, units if known, and vertex/face counts.
Fails gracefully when scan extras are missing.
Does not introduce any 3D execution path.

session_log.summary: Created initial software-frontload task tracker. Hardware is not ready; focus is on mock-safe and fake-serial work.
